title: Papaver sokolovskajae

This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the family. Papaver sokolovskajae is further classified in the order Ranunculales, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics. intro: Papaver sokolovskajae, also known as the Sokolov's poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of respiratory issues, inflammation, and anxiety, thanks to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Papaver sokolovskajae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal activity. In traditional medicine, Papaver sokolovskajae is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried extract, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create various herbal preparations.
